# Children Game UX Design

Use this skill when designing or reviewing child-friendly game UI for this English learning app.

## Principles

- Target age: 6-7 years old.
- Use large text, high contrast, simple choices, and clear visual goals.
- Keep primary touch targets at least 56px high.
- Prefer one main action per card.
- Use low-pressure feedback: encourage retry instead of showing failure.
- Make rewards visible immediately with animation and sound-ready hooks.
- Keep parent/admin features visually secondary.

## Game Loop Checklist

1. Show today's goal clearly.
2. Give instant feedback after each speech attempt.
3. Limit daily star earnings to avoid economy inflation.
4. Turn stars into care actions: sun, rain, feed, seeds.
5. Make garden growth visual and tactile.
6. Show warehouse counts with emoji and big numbers.
7. Use reward pacing: small reward each day, character unlock about every 10 days.
8. Preserve progress across sessions.

## Visual Style

- Pastel cartoon palette: strawberry pink, cream, sky blue, grass green, warm yellow.
- Rounded panels, soft shadows, bouncy micro-animations.
- Use original cute mascots only; do not copy copyrighted characters.

## Accessibility

- Support `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- Buttons must have text, not emoji only.
- Use readable Chinese labels for children and parents.
- Avoid time pressure for speech practice.
